items.question: What is the 3D Figure Design AI workflow?
items.answer: 3D Figure Design is an AI-powered workflow for creating 3D-style figure visuals—ranging from stylized characters to product mascots and collectible figures. It focuses on generating high-quality concept images, not full 3D meshes or rigged models.

items.question: What kind of input works best for 3D figure design?
items.answer: You can start from text-only briefs, image references, or a mix of both. Clear descriptions of personality, proportions, clothing style, and materials—plus rough sketches or photos—help the AI generate more accurate figure concepts.

items.question: Can this workflow replace traditional 3D modeling?
items.answer: No. The workflow is designed for 3D-style concept images, not production-ready 3D assets. Use it to explore figure directions quickly, then pass the chosen visuals to 3D artists or modeling tools for final production.

items.question: Who typically uses the 3D Figure Design template?
items.answer: It is ideal for toy and collectible brands, game and entertainment studios, brand and marketing teams, and freelance illustrators who need fast iterations on figure concepts before committing to full pipelines.

items.question: What types of 3D figure styles can I explore?
items.answer: You can experiment with toy-like, realistic, stylized, chibi, or collectible figure aesthetics. Prompts can also specify materials, finishes, and lighting moods (matte, glossy, metallic, soft lighting, and more).

items.question: How can I use the outputs from this workflow?
items.answer: You can use generated figures in internal pitch decks, brand asset explorations, packaging mockups, and as reference sheets for downstream 3D modeling or detailed illustration work.

items.question: Is commercial use of generated figures allowed?
items.answer: Commercial use depends on the terms and policies of OpenCreator and connected AI providers. Before using outputs in final products or large campaigns, check the latest licensing and usage guidelines in the product documentation.
